summary: The /chat threading fix closes the original drop-at-boundary bug, but explicit classifications are still trusted too loosely: out-of-vocabulary string values are honored instead of falling back to the classifier, so malformed hints can still misroute turns.

findings.title: Explicit routing hints honor arbitrary string taskType/tier values instead of clamping or falling back
findings.recommendation: Validate explicit `classification.taskType` and `classification.tier` against the router vocabularies before honoring them, or reuse the same clamping/fallback path as `parseClassification()`. As written, `{taskType:"bogus", tier:"T2-deep"}` is treated as honored and routes via matrix defaults/column defaults rather than reclassifying the message, contradicting the intended absent/malformed fallback behavior. Add a regression test that posts an out-of-vocabulary explicit classification and asserts classifier fallback.
